observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the MT agreed to prepare the RCI and the schedule of unreleased/unclaimed checks at the end of the reporting period and submit thereafter to the Municipal Accountant.

We also recommended and Management agreed that the: a) MT and Municipal Accountant to reconcile CIB accounts and readily act on reconciling items for adjustment by the LGU as appearing in the monthly bank reconciliation statements such as the debit memos, credit memos, and stale checks including the long-standing reconciling items; and b) MT cause to (i) submit the monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) of all accountable forms used by the MTO to the AT not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month; (ii) submit regularly the Report of Checks Issued and maintain a Check Register; and (iii) monitor regularly the recording in the cashbook and reconcile monthly with the report in the Accounting Office.

We recommended and the MTO agreed to ensure the timely submission of the Certified List of Taxpayers to the MAO.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, a) reconcile the discrepancies between the Treasury and Accounting records; and b) conduct timely and regular reconciliation of collections of RPT and SET.

We recommended and the MT agreed to continuously enforce the remedies for the collection of delinquent RPT as provided in Section 256 of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the inclusion of the different Departments to conduct complete physical count and the define the scope of their participation in the Physical Inventory Plan.

We also recommended and the designated GSO/MT and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare and maintain updated property cards to enable reconciliation with the general ledger balances and the inventory report.

We recommended and Management agreed that the: i) Municipal Assessor expedite the titling of parcels of land and road lots with a total assessed value of ₱1,546,240.00 in the name of the Municipality; and ii) Municipal Accountant recognize the same in the financial statements.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, agreed to determine the deemed cost of the unrecorded parcels of land and road lots with a total assessed value of ₱23,872,020.00 and recognize the same in the financial statements.

We recommended and the MBO, in coordination with the MAO, agreed to classify budget allocations for procurement or construction of PPE items to their appropriate classification.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct account balances of PPE classification and other related accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reclassify the semi-expendable properties in accordance with COA Circular No. 2024-006 and effect the necessary adjustment in the books, if warranted.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. Inventory Committee i) establish formal procedures for the annual assessment of unserviceable properties; (ii) submit the result of assessment to the MAO; and (iii) Initiate disposal of said properties; and
b. MAO recognize and disclose asset impairment at reporting date.

We recommended and the GSO agreed to –
a) Prepare the Property Inventory Form as basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage over all insurable assets, properties and interests with the GSIS; and
b) take necessary steps to register and secure insurance coverage for all government-owned motor vehicles in the Municipality, as well as other insurable assets.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to include in the year-end closing of books the classification of current and non-current liabilities and reflect the same in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to conduct one-time cleansing of dormant accounts in accordance with Item 5.6 of COA Circular No. 2023-008 to ensure the accurate presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the –
a. HRMO to provide computation on the accumulated earned leave credits of personnel and forward the same to the Municipal Accountant for the setting up of Leave Benefits Payable for CY 2025;
b. Municipal Accountant to (a) set up Leave Benefits Payable using the accumulated leave credits to be provided by the HRMO; and (b) accrue the earned leaves using the appropriate accounts; and
c. MBO to (a) plan the full funding of leave credits; and (b) realign the OPB-Monetization of Leave Benefits to TLB.

We recommended and the MTO agreed to coordinate with DICT to adjust the amount of gross sales tax in the iBPLS to reflect the appropriate tax due to be collected.

We recommended and the MTO agreed to -
a) issue notices to delinquent taxpayers; and
b) conduct regular information campaigns to educate business owners on their tax obligations.

We also recommended and Management agreed to consider the updating of its Municipal Revenue Code to potentially increase the revenues of the Municipality which could have been used to defray expenses and for implementation of additional PPAs that would benefit the public.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to enhance the completeness and compliance of the Notes to the Financial Statements by utilizing a standardized disclosure checklist, serving as a guide to ensure that all required disclosures are accurately and consistently presented in accordance with IPSAS.

We recommended and Management agreed that the i) GSO, MSWDO and Office of the Mayor commence the awarding of procurement only upon the issuance of an approved obligation; ii) the preparer/reviewer of the contract ensure that the contract explicitly states the contract amount and that such amount is aligned with the approved budget and does not exceed the allowable funds specified therein; and iii) LFC to strengthen document tracking controls to ensure adherence to specified processing timeframes and to establish clear accountability at each stage of document handling.

We recommended and the MPDO, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and the Local Development Council, agreed to continue to strengthen development planning and project prioritization to ensure that the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF is compliant with the prescribed criteria and respond effectively to the community’s evolving needs.

We recommended and the MAO agreed to draw entries at year-end to accrue the unexpended balance of LDRRMF to Special Trust Fund.

We recommended and the MDRRMO agreed to –
a. conduct a comprehensive planning for the formulation of PPAs under LDRRMF in coordination with the other departments and government agencies.
b. devise PPAs which will significantly contribute in ensuring the protection and welfare of the people during disasters or emergencies; and
c. ensure that the LDRRMF is optimally utilized pursuant to existing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended and the MSWDO agreed to; i) maintain records of the receipt and distribution of welfare goods; and ii) include in the distribution sheet the list of specific items and quantity received by the recipients.

We also recommended and the MDRRMO agreed to ensure that the medicines and other medical supplies are for disaster-related injuries and illnesses in compliance with Item 5.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended and the Local Finance Committee (LFC) agreed to (i) post the quarterly reports on fund utilization and status of program/project implementation; and (ii) send written notices to various oversight agencies as required by Section 5.0 of LBC No 151 for posting and reporting of the implementation of the priority projects.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to require the submission of the Construction Safety and Health Program (CSHP) prior to endorsing any progress billings to the Municipal Accountant for payment.

We recommended and the BAC Chair agreed to a) impose monitoring controls with the end-view of ensuring compliance with procurement posting and reporting requirements; and b) subject the head of the BAC Secretariat a proper seminar/training on the rules and regulations of the IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to i) to refrain from charging outright to appropriate asset account the agency assets and public infrastructures under construction in order to present fairly the account balances of the Municipality, pursuant to Section 50 of NGAS, Volume 1; and ii) to prepare the correct adjustment relating to the provision of depreciation expense for the building and hospital and health center.

We recommended and Management agreed to a) ensure that revisions or realignments in the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) are supported with a formal written notification to the concerned DILG Office from the LCE; and b) review and consider the revision of the GAD Code to ensure that GAD mandates are in alignment with the GAD objectives of addressing gender issues towards gender equality and women empowerment.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the GFPS to formulate the agency’s GAD Agenda in accordance with PCW MC No. 2018-04.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the GAD Agenda is regularly reviewed, evaluated, and submitted to PCW, with the necessary progress and end-term reports to support monitoring and technical assistance and to strengthen the alignment of all PAPs with its GAD goals.

We recommended and the GFPS agreed to prepare the required documentation to support the submission of GAD AR.

We recommended and the Human Resources Management Office agreed to:
a) prioritize reorganization of the Municipality’s Organizational Structure and filling the approved plantilla positions; and b) consider appointing qualified existing Job Order personnel to the vacant positions, subject to civil service laws, rules, and regulations, as well as the authorized Personnel Services limitations.

We recommended and the HRMO, in coordination with all concerned departments, agreed to review the duties and responsibilities of all JO personnel and consider retaining only those deemed essential in the Municipality’s operation with the end-view of creating fiscal space that may support salary enhancement.
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipality exert more effort to reconcile/trace the prior years unreconciled balance.

observations.recommendation:

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant requested additional personnel who will prepare the subsidiary ledgers.




The Municipal Treasurer will coordinate with the Provincial Treasurer with regard to the publication of delinquent taxpayers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to assess its existing organizational structure so that the ideal number of plantilla positions will be established, prioritize filling up of vacant plantilla positions, thereby, avoiding the hiring of excessive number of job-order personnel. 

Likewise, we recommended that the LGU adhere to the policies and guidelines in hiring of personnel under job-orders as provided in CSC Resolution No. 020790.
observations.managementAction: The Management had already finished the proposed re-organization, however, it is not yet submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan for approval.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the concerned Departments to 

a) impose liquidated damages amounting to ₱1,143,354.12 to the Contractor of Two Storey Standard PAGCOR Multi-Purpose Evacuation Center in accordance with Section 8.1 of Annex E of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (RIRR) of Republic Act (RA) 9184; and 


b) submit the logbook from January 25 to September 23, 2022 of the said PAGCOR project in adherence with Item Nos. 2 and 3 of DPWH Department Order No. 216, series of 2000.
observations.managementAction: This has been noted by the Management, corresponding amount will be deducted from the retention/ recoupment of the contractor based on actual delays on the target completion of the project.
This has been echoed to all Departments especially the Accounting Office to ensure that all supporting documents with regard to disbursements are all complete.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and  Management agreed  through the GAD focal person to  

(a) submit a copy of the Annual GPB to the COA Audit Team within five workings days from the receipt of the DILG endorsed plan; and

(b) submit the annual GAD Accomplishment Report within five working days from the end of January of the preceding year with the (i) brief summary of the reported program or project; (ii) copies of reported policy issuances;                              (iii) results of HGDG tests, and 
(iv) actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations.
observations.managementAction: This has been noted by the GAD Focal Person.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to 

(a) direct concerned offices to 
(i) review and analyze the loans payable and debt servicing capacity of the Municipality to manage and accomplish development projects without sacrificing other priority expenses;        


(ii) undertake feasibility studies to make sure that the income generating projects will produce revenue as intended; and 

(b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to classify correctly the Loans Payable in the Statement of Financial Position as current or non-current liabilities.
observations.managementAction:
The Local Chief Executive will convene the Local Finance Committee to review and analyze further the debt servicing capacity of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere to the above-cited regulations to prevent reduction of appropriation for the current year and avoid likelihood of possible overdraft and that valid unpaid obligations be recorded as Payables to validate their payments in the succeeding years.

Treasurer to prepare a schedule of unreleased/unclaimed checks at the end of the
reporting period and submit thereafter to the Municipal Accountant; and (b)
Municipal Accountant to post adjustment in the books of accounts that correspond
to the unreleased or unclaimed checks at every end of the reporting period.

1.22 We also recommended and Management agreed to instruct the: a) Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile cash in bank accounts and readily
act on reconciling items for adjustment by the LGU as appearing in the monthly
bank reconciliation statements such as the debit memos, credit memos, and stale
checks; and b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) submit the monthly Report of
Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) of all accountable forms used by
the Municipal Treasury to the Audit Team not later than the fifth day of the
ensuing month; (ii) submit regularly the Report of Checks Issued and maintain a
Check Register for proper monitoring of checks issued; (iii) coordinate with the
Landbank of the Philippines for the erroneously credited amount of ₱80,322.60
from General Fund – LBP Account no. 0442-1040-42 for proper recording and
adjustment to be taken in the books of account; and (iv) monitor regularly the
recording in the cashbook and reconcile monthly with the report in the
Accounting Office.

2.19 We reiterated prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the corresponding subsidiary
ledgers (SLs) to monitor tax collections and delinquencies in compliance with
Section 4 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I and the Abstract of Real
Property Tax in accordance with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS,
Volume I; and
b) take appropriate action against taxpayers with substantial deficiencies in
accordance with Sections 254 and 256 of RA No. 7160.

3.38 We recommended and Management agreed that the:
(a) LCE (i) reconstitute the Inventory Committee to provide adequate
number of personnel to be able to prepare the Physical Inventory Plan and
complete the physical inventory in adherence with COA Circular 2020-006,
by following the general and procedural guidelines stated in the said Circular
including disposition and recording of the missing PPEs; (ii) request for
authority for the derecognition of missing items, duly supported with
appropriate documentation, if accountability/ responsibility over the missing
PPE could not be pinpointed after investigation as required under Section 7
of COA Circular 2020-006; and (iii) submit request for relief from
49
accountability for the lost PPE items in adherence with COA Circular No.
92-386 and 2009 Revised Rules of Procedure of the Commission on Audit
otherwise, demand shall be made from the accountable official to produce
the item or pay the depreciated replacement cost thereof;
(b) Management allocate fund for the expenses to secure copies of the title of
the declared real properties and assign a focal person who will focus on the
requirements needed to facilitate the immediate transfer of titles of the
parcels of land under the name of the Municipality;
(c) Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant request and assign,
as the case may be, personnel who will prepare and maintain updated
property cards to enable reconciliation with the general ledger balances and
the inventory report;
(d) OIC - General Services Officer prepare motor vehicle profile indicating
the date of acquisition, model/make/type and cost to determine those which
need comprehensive insurance;
(e) OIC - General Services Officer with the assistance of the Inventory
Committee to (a) consider undertaking a one-time cleansing of PPE account
balances and strictly comply with the guidelines and procedures enumerated
in COA Circular No. 2020-006; and (b) pursue the disposal of all
unserviceable properties; and
(f) Municipal Accountant to update and provide depreciation to the affected
PPE accounts in order to present their reasonable carrying values in the
financial statements in conformity with the provisions of IPSAS 17.

4.6 We recommended and Management agreed to (a) adhere to the provisions of
the Joint Circular pertaining to the limitation of release of cash advances on the
requirements for three months in line with the Physical and Financial Plan of the
Municipality to avoid the possible misuse or misappropriation of the fund; (b)
submit the relative documents for Cash Advance for Confidential Fund within seven
days after the release of the check; (c) provide the necessary and correct supporting
documents particularly copy of the transmittal letter of LR submitted to ICFAU; and (d)
comply with the provisions of the Joint Circular that all cash advances for CF/IF shall be
liquidated at year-end.

5.26 We recommended and Management agreed to –
a) trace the causes for discrepancies between the amounts reported in trial
balance and amount presented in the financial statements and effect
necessary reconciliation and adjustments, as warranted;
b) revert the unused balance to the Source Agency upon completion of the
project of related trust fund accounts in compliance with Item 6.7 of COA
Circular No. 94-013;
c) consider one-time cleansing of dormant accounts in accordance with Item 5.6
of COA Circular No. 2023-008; and
d) submit the required documents to support and justify the payments made for
HEA.

5.27 We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw the
following adjusting entries:
Particulars: To transfer the funds received from PAGCOR from Due to
NGAs to Due to GOCCs
Account Title Debit Credit
Due to NGAs ₱4,779,226.75
Due to GOCCs ₱4,779,226.75

6.14 We recommended and Management agreed to direct the (a) MPDO, in
coordination with the Municipal Engineer and Bids and Awards Committee, to
expedite the implementation and completion of the two identified priority projects
before the end of validity period to prevent the reversion of fund to National
Treasury; and (b) Local Finance Committee (LFC) to (i) prepare and post the
quarterly reports on fund utilization and status of program/project implementation;
and (ii) send written notices to various oversight agencies as required by Section 5.0
of LBC No 151 for posting and reporting of the implementation of the priority
projects.

7.7 We reiterated prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to
adhere to the above-cited regulations to prevent reduction of appropriation for the
current year and avoid likelihood of possible overdraft and that valid unpaid
obligations be recorded as Payables to validate their payments in the succeeding
years.

7.8 We recommended and the Municipal Accounting Office agreed to earmark
accounts payable for expenditures incurred in the current year which will be paid in
the following year to render account balances reliable.

8.17 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to –
a) reconcile the accounting records with that of the lending institution and
effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the loans in the
Subsidiary Ledger for individual loan accounts being maintained by the
Accounting Office; and
b) reclassify the portion of the loans amounting to ₱28,130,351.73 which is
expected to be settled within 12 months after the reporting date as current
liability to present fairly the account balance in the financial statements.

8.18 We also recommended and Management agreed to conduct cost-benefit
analysis in the availment of loans for projects which could be financed using the
current year’s appropriation to save payment for interest expenses, thereby
increasing funds available for the implementation of other projects of the Municipality.

9.21 We recommended and Management agreed to undertake the updating of its
Municipal Revenue Code to potentially increase the revenues of the Municipality
which could have been used to defray expenses and for implementation of additional
PPAs that would benefit the public.

9.22 We also recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to coordinate with the Department of Information and Communications
Technology (DICT) in relation to business taxes which led to the erroneous
calculated tax due which resulted to an under collection of ₱30,618.78, without
prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of Charge, if warranted.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipality
continue its efforts to further assess and provide solutions to the
pandemic.

We also recommended and the Management agreed that the (a) OIC-GSO
issue ICS on semi-expendable supplies in compliance with COA Circular 2005-002;
(b) Municipal Accountant ensure completeness of the supporting documents before
effecting payment; (c) concerned departments (i) submit all the necessary
supporting documents stated in the MOA for the fund transfers from NGAs; and
(ii) comply with the posting requirements of LBC No. 125 and GPPB Circular 02-
2021.

We also recommendedthat the Local Finance Committesubmit the report
on fund utilization and status of implementation using the prescribed format to the
DBM and to the Office of the COA in compliance with DBM LBC No. 139.

We recommended that the:

(a) LCE create an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of personnel to be able to complete the physical inventory in three (3) months or less in adherence with COA Circular 2020-006.  The Inventory Committee are advised to follow the general and procedural guidelines stated in the said Circular including disposition and recording of the missing PPEs.

(b) Management allocate fund for the expenses in securing copies of the title of the declared real properties and assigned a focal person who will focus on the requirements needed to enable the immediate transfer of title of the land declared under the name of the Municipality; and

(c) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant request and assigned personnel who will prepare and maintain updated property cards to enable reconciliation with the general ledger balances and the inventory report.
observations.managementAction: An inventory committee was created thru issuance of Executive Order No. 67 Series of 2021 dated October 25, 2021 to reorganize the committee for the proper conduct of inventory of PPEs.
observations.reason: The Executive Order did not take into consideration the COA Circular 2020-006 or the Guidelines and Procedures for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to set up RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year and prepare the corresponding Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) to monitor tax collections and delinquencies in compliance with Sections 4 and 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I.

4.3	We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant submit the Cash Advance for Confidential Fund within seven days after the release of the check; (b) Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the Statement of itemized POP includes programs and projects which are also included in the Annual Budget and Physical and Financial Plan and compute the 30% of the allowed Confidential Fund base on the POP only; and (c) Management submit the Certified copy of the minutes of the meeting evidencing the 2/3 votes of the Local Peace and Order Council approving the POP.

5.6	We recommended that Management instruct their contractors to comply with COA Circular No. 2014-003 pertaining to Information and Publicity of Program/Projects/Activities of Government Agencies so that policy on full disclosure is adopted and conduct Material Testing on the applicable infrastructure projects in accordance with Section 2.2.3 of COA Circular No. 2013-004.

ensure full implementation/operation of the existing composting facility of the municipality and of the fourteen (14) barangay’s MRF to help in waste diversion of the municipality.
continue all its efforts and initiative in maintaining the operations of a   centralized MRF and its plans and programs incorporated in the ten-year Solid Waste Management Plan in accordance with the RA No. 9003.
